_comment: Answer-quality eval: questions + rubric key-points a correct answer must cover. `npm run answer-eval` runs each question through the real /api/chat pipeline (grounding + Claude), then an LLM judge scores correctness/completeness/grounded against the rubric. Measures ANSWER quality (what users feel), not just retrieval recall. Costs 2 API calls per question — run occasionally, not in the fast test loop.

cases.q: How do I define a table with a primary key in Harper?
key_points: tables are defined with a GraphQL schema (SDL) using the @table directive
the primary key is marked with the @primaryKey directive on an attribute
a concrete example, e.g. `type Dog @table { id: ID @primaryKey }`

cases.q: How do I cache data from an external source in Harper?
key_points: define a cache table (a @table, typically with an expiration/TTL)
wrap the external source in a custom Resource class (extending Resource)
connect them so Harper fetches on miss (e.g. sourcedFrom, or overriding get())

cases.q: How do I write a custom REST endpoint in Harper?
key_points: define a class that extends Resource in a jsResource file
the exported resource is served over REST at a path

cases.q: How do I subscribe to real-time changes over MQTT?
key_points: Harper supports MQTT (pub/sub) as a protocol
you subscribe to a topic/table to receive real-time updates
changes to records are published to subscribers

cases.q: How do I run TypeScript in Harper without a build step?
key_points: Harper runs .ts files directly via type stripping (no transpile/build)
requires a recent Node (22.6+) / the jsResource glob can target .ts
local imports must use explicit .ts extensions

cases.q: How do I load environment variables from a .env file?
key_points: use the loadEnv plugin/config in config.yaml
the variables become available to the application
